content.text: **Module 2: Enrollment & Ordering**. I'll create entities, repositories, services, controllers, and tests. Let me use agents to parallelize entity creation and service logic:

content.name: Agent
content.arguments:
{"description": "Build enrollment/order backend", "subagent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "You need to create the backend files for Module 2: Enrollment & Ordering in the CHECC project at E:/Hale/Coding/Eaglepoint/Task-54/repo. The monorepo structure, shared types, and auth module are already in place. DO write actual code files.

Create these files:

## 1. TypeORM Entities
**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/catalog-service.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('catalog_services')
Fields: id (uuid PK), code (unique varchar), name, description, basePrice (numeric 10,2 mapped from base_price), category, isActive, maxSeats (nullable int), createdAt
```

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/enrollment.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('enrollments')
Fields: id (uuid PK), patientId (FK users), status (DRAFT|SUBMITTED|ACTIVE|REJECTED|CANCELED), enrollmentDate (date nullable), notes (text), createdAt, updatedAt, submittedAt (nullable)
Relations: @ManyToOne to UserEntity (patient), @OneToMany to EnrollmentServiceLineEntity
```

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/enrollment-service-line.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('enrollment_service_lines')
Fields: id, enrollmentId (FK), serviceId (FK), quantity, createdAt
Relations: @ManyToOne to EnrollmentEntity and CatalogServiceEntity
```

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/seat-reservation.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('seat_reservations')
Fields: id, serviceId (FK), enrollmentId (FK), reservedAt, expiresAt, status (HELD|CONFIRMED|RELEASED)
```

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/order.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('orders')
Fields: id, orderNumber (unique), enrollmentId (FK), patientId (FK users), status (PENDING_PAYMENT|PAID|REFUNDED|CANCELED), subtotal, discountTotal, finalTotal, createdAt, updatedAt, autoCancelAt
Relations: @OneToMany to OrderLineEntity
```

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/order-line.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('order_lines')
Fields: id, orderId (FK), serviceId (FK), quantity, unitPrice (numeric), discountAmount, lineTotal, discountReason (nullable text)
Relations: @ManyToOne to OrderEntity and CatalogServiceEntity
```

## 2. Services (Use Cases)
**File: `apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/enrollment.service.ts`**
Responsibilities:
- `create(patientId, input)` - Create a DRAFT enrollment with service lines
- `update(enrollmentId, userId, input)` - Update a DRAFT enrollment (object-level auth: must be the patient's own)
- `findById(id)` - Get enrollment with service lines
- `findByPatient(patientId, page, limit)` - Paginated list
- `submit(enrollmentId, userId)` - Transition DRAFT→SUBMITTED, create seat reservations (60-min TTL for services with maxSeats), create Order with order lines (snapshot prices from catalog), set autoCancelAt = now + 30min
- `activate(enrollmentId)` - Transition to ACTIVE (called when payment completes)
- `cancel(enrollmentId)` - Transition to CANCELED, release seat reservations

Key logic:
- Order number generation: `ORD-{YYYYMMDD}-{random 6 digits}`
- On submit: snapshot current base_price from catalog_services into order_lines.unit_price
- Seat reservation: check available seats = maxSeats - count(HELD or CONFIRMED reservations for service)
- Object-level auth: patients can only see/edit their own enrollments

**File: `apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/order.service.ts`**
Responsibilities:
- `findById(id)` - Get order with lines
- `findByPatient(patientId, page, limit)` - Paginated list
- `cancel(orderId)` - Cancel order and revert enrollment to DRAFT, release seats

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/scheduling/order-timeout.service.ts`**
- Cron job: every 5 minutes (`@Cron('0 */5 * * * *')`)
- Find orders where status='PENDING_PAYMENT' AND auto_cancel_at < NOW()
- Cancel them, revert enrollment to DRAFT, release seat reservations
- Also release expired seat reservations (expires_at < NOW() AND status='HELD')

## 3. Controllers
**File: `apps/api/src/api/controllers/enrollment.controller.ts`**
- `POST /enrollments` - Create (Patient only via @Roles)
- `GET /enrollments` - List own enrollments (Patient), or all (Staff/Admin)
- `GET /enrollments/:id` - Get by ID (owner or Staff/Admin)
- `PUT /enrollments/:id` - Update draft (owner only)
- `POST /enrollments/:id/submit` - Submit enrollment
- `POST /enrollments/:id/cancel` - Cancel enrollment
Uses ZodValidate pipe with createEnrollmentSchema and updateEnrollmentSchema from @checc/shared/schemas/enrollment.schema

**File: `apps/api/src/api/controllers/order.controller.ts`**
- `GET /orders` - List orders for patient or all (Staff/Admin)
- `GET /orders/:id` - Get order by ID (owner or Staff/Admin)
- `POST /orders/:id/cancel` - Cancel order

**File: `apps/api/src/api/controllers/catalog.controller.ts`**
- `GET /catalog` - List active catalog services (public for authenticated users)
- `GET /catalog/:id` - Get by ID

## 4. Module
**File: `apps/api/src/api/modules/enrollment.module.ts`**
Wire up all the above. Import TypeOrmModule.forFeature with all entities. Export services.

## 5. Update app.module.ts
Add EnrollmentModule to imports in `apps/api/src/app.module.ts`

## 6. Tests
**File: `apps/api/test/enrollment.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
- Create enrollment with service lines
- Update enrollment (only DRAFT status)
- Submit enrollment: creates order, reserves seats
- Submit enrollment: rejects if seat unavailable
- Submit enrollment: rejects if not DRAFT
- Cancel enrollment: releases seats, reverts status
- Object-level auth: patient can only access own enrollments

**File: `apps/api/test/order-timeout.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
- Cancels overdue PENDING_PAYMENT orders
- Releases expired seat reservations
- Does not cancel non-overdue orders

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- All column names use snake_case in DB, camelCase in TypeScript with @Column({ name: 'snake_case' })
- Use `@checc/shared` imports for types and schemas
- Inject repositories via TypeORM @InjectRepository
- Use the WinstonLogger for logging
- All services should check ownership where relevant (patient_id matches current user)
- Follow the existing patterns from auth module (auth.service.ts, auth.controller.ts, auth.module.ts)
- The enrollment module should import AuthModule to access USER_REPOSITORY if needed"}

- `update(enrollmentId, userId, input)` - Update a DRAFT enrollment (object-level auth: must be the patient's own)
- `findById(id)` - Get enrollment with service lines
- `findByPatient(patientId, page, limit)` - Paginated list
- `submit(enrollmentId, userId)` - Transition DRAFT→SUBMITTED, create seat reservations (60-min TTL for services with maxSeats), create Order with order lines (snapshot prices from catalog), set autoCancelAt = now + 30min
- `activate(enrollmentId)` - Transition to ACTIVE (called when payment completes)
- `cancel(enrollmentId)` - Transition to CANCELED, release seat reservations

Key logic:
- Order number generation: `ORD-{YYYYMMDD}-{random 6 digits}`
- On submit: snapshot current base_price from catalog_services into order_lines.unit_price
- Seat reservation: check available seats = maxSeats - count(HELD or CONFIRMED reservations for service)
- Object-level auth: patients can only see/edit their own enrollments

**File: `apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/order.service.ts`**
Responsibilities:
- `findById(id)` - Get order with lines
- `findByPatient(patientId, page, limit)` - Paginated list
- `cancel(orderId)` - Cancel order and revert enrollment to DRAFT, release seats

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/scheduling/order-timeout.service.ts`**
- Cron job: every 5 minutes (`@Cron('0 */5 * * * *')`)
- Find orders where status='PENDING_PAYMENT' AND auto_cancel_at < NOW()
- Cancel them, revert enrollment to DRAFT, release seat reservations
- Also release expired seat reservations (expires_at < NOW() AND status='HELD')

## 3. Controllers
**File: `apps/api/src/api/controllers/enrollment.controller.ts`**
- `POST /enrollments` - Create (Patient only via @Roles)
- `GET /enrollments` - List own enrollments (Patient), or all (Staff/Admin)
- `GET /enrollments/:id` - Get by ID (owner or Staff/Admin)
- `PUT /enrollments/:id` - Update draft (owner only)
- `POST /enrollments/:id/submit` - Submit enrollment
- `POST /enrollments/:id/cancel` - Cancel enrollment
Uses ZodValidate pipe with createEnrollmentSchema and updateEnrollmentSchema from @checc/shared/schemas/enrollment.schema

**File: `apps/api/src/api/controllers/order.controller.ts`**
- `GET /orders` - List orders for patient or all (Staff/Admin)
- `GET /orders/:id` - Get order by ID (owner or Staff/Admin)
- `POST /orders/:id/cancel` - Cancel order

**File: `apps/api/src/api/controllers/catalog.controller.ts`**
- `GET /catalog` - List active catalog services (public for authenticated users)
- `GET /catalog/:id` - Get by ID

## 4. Module
**File: `apps/api/src/api/modules/enrollment.module.ts`**
Wire up all the above. Import TypeOrmModule.forFeature with all entities. Export services.

## 5. Update app.module.ts
Add EnrollmentModule to imports in `apps/api/src/app.module.ts`

## 6. Tests
**File: `apps/api/test/enrollment.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
- Create enrollment with service lines
- Update enrollment (only DRAFT status)
- Submit enrollment: creates order, reserves seats
- Submit enrollment: rejects if seat unavailable
- Submit enrollment: rejects if not DRAFT
- Cancel enrollment: releases seats, reverts status
- Object-level auth: patient can only access own enrollments

**File: `apps/api/test/order-timeout.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
- Cancels overdue PENDING_PAYMENT orders
- Releases expired seat reservations
- Does not cancel non-overdue orders

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- All column names use snake_case in DB, camelCase in TypeScript with @Column({ name: 'snake_case' })
- Use `@checc/shared` imports for types and schemas
- Inject repositories via TypeORM @InjectRepository
- Use the WinstonLogger for logging
- All services should check ownership where relevant (patient_id matches current user)
- Follow the existing patterns from auth module (auth.service.ts, auth.controller.ts, auth.module.ts)
- The enrollment module should import AuthModule to access USER_REPOSITORY if needed"}

content.text: **Module 3: Pricing & Promotion Engine**. This is the most complex business logic — the deterministic pricing engine with exclusion groups and immutable audit trail.

content.name: Agent
content.arguments:
{"description": "Build pricing engine module", "subagent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "Create the Pricing & Promotion Engine (Module 3) for the CHECC project at E:/Hale/Coding/Eaglepoint/Task-54/repo. The existing codebase has Module 0 (infrastructure), Module 1 (auth), and Module 2 (enrollment/ordering) complete. DO write actual code files.

Read the existing files first to understand patterns:
- apps/api/src/app.module.ts (to see how modules are imported)
- apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/order.entity.ts
- apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/order-line.entity.ts
- libs/shared/src/types/pricing.types.ts
- libs/shared/src/schemas/pricing.schema.ts

Then create these files:

## 1. TypeORM Entity
**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/pricing-rule.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('pricing_rules')
Fields: id, name, description, ruleType (rule_type), priorityLevel (priority_level), value (numeric 10,4), minQuantity, applicableServiceIds (uuid array), applicableCategories (varchar array), exclusionGroup, validFrom, validUntil, isActive, createdBy (FK users), createdAt
```

**File: `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/discount-audit.entity.ts`**
```
@Entity('discount_audit_trail')
IMMUTABLE — no update methods should be exposed
Fields: id, orderId (FK), orderLineId (FK nullable), pricingRuleId (FK nullable), originalPrice, discountAmount, finalPrice, reasoning (JSONB), computedAt
```

## 2. The Pricing Engine (MOST CRITICAL FILE)
**File: `apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/pricing-engine.ts`**

This must implement the EXACT algorithm:

```typescript
class PricingEngine {
  /**
   * Computes the best price for a single order line.
   * 
   * Algorithm:
   * 1. FILTER active rules where:
   *    - is_active = true
   *    - valid_from <= now <= valid_until
   *    - applicable_service_ids is null OR contains line.serviceId
   *    - applicable_categories is null OR contains line.category
   *    - line.quantity >= min_quantity
   * 
   * 2. SORT by priority_level ASC (stable sort, tie-break by rule ID)
   * 
   * 3. GROUP by exclusion_group (null = standalone, always applied)
   * 
   * 4. For each exclusion group:
   *    - Compute the discount each member rule would give
   *    - Select the rule yielding MAXIMUM discount for the patient
   *    - Tie-break: lower priority_level, then lower rule ID
   * 
   * 5. Standalone rules (no exclusion group): all are selected
   * 
   * 6. APPLY selected rules in priority order:
   *    - percentage_off: discount = unit_price * quantity * value / 100
   *    - fixed_off: discount = value * quantity
   *    - fixed_price: discount = (unit_price - value) * quantity
   *    - buy_x_get_y: discount = floor(quantity / (min_quantity + 1)) * unit_price * value
   * 
   * 7. Cap total discount at line subtotal (never negative price)
   * 
   * 8. Build reasoning object with full evaluation trace
   * 
   * 9. Return { discountAmount, finalPrice, reasoning }
   */
  computeLineDiscount(
    line: { serviceId: string; category: string; unitPrice: number; quantity: number },
    rules: PricingRuleEntity[],
    now: Date
  ): LineDiscountResult
  
  /**
   * Compute discounts for an entire order (all lines).
   */
  computeOrderDiscounts(
    lines: OrderLineInput[],
    rules: PricingRuleEntity[],
    now?: Date
  ): OrderDiscountResult
}
```

The engine must be a pure function (no DB access) so it's highly testable. It receives rules and lines, returns results.

## 3. Pricing Service
**File: `apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/pricing.service.ts`**
- `createRule(input, userId)` - Create pricing rule (Admin only)
- `updateRule(id, input)` - Update pricing rule
- `deleteRule(id)` - Soft-delete (set isActive=false)
- `listRules(page, limit, activeOnly?)` - Paginated list
- `computeForOrder(orderLines)` - Fetches active rules, calls PricingEngine, returns results
- `applyToOrder(orderId)` - Compute + persist to order_lines + write discount_audit_trail (IMMUTABLE insert)

## 4. Controller
**File: `apps/api/src/api/controllers/pricing.controller.ts`**
- `POST /pricing/rules` - Create rule (Admin only)
- `PUT /pricing/rules/:id` - Update rule (Admin only)
- `DELETE /pricing/rules/:id` - Deactivate rule (Admin only)
- `GET /pricing/rules` - List rules (Admin)
- `POST /pricing/compute` - Preview pricing for a set of lines (any authenticated user)
- `GET /pricing/audit/:orderId` - Get discount audit trail for an order

## 5. Module
**File: `apps/api/src/api/modules/pricing.module.ts`**
Wire everything up. Import TypeOrmModule.forFeature for PricingRuleEntity, DiscountAuditEntity, OrderEntity, OrderLineEntity, CatalogServiceEntity.

## 6. Update app.module.ts
Add PricingModule to imports.

## 7. Tests (CRITICAL - this is the core business logic)
**File: `apps/api/test/pricing-engine.spec.ts`**

This file must be comprehensive because the pricing engine is the most complex piece of logic:

Test cases:
1. No applicable rules → zero discount
2. Single percentage_off rule applies correctly
3. Single fixed_off rule applies correctly
4. Single fixed_price rule applies correctly
5. buy_x_get_y rule: quantity=3, buy 2 get 1 → 1 free item
6. buy_x_get_y rule: quantity=1 (below min) → no discount
7. Exclusion group: two mutually exclusive rules, picks max discount
8. Exclusion group tie-break: equal discount, picks lower priority
9. Standalone rules: both apply (no exclusion group)
10. Mixed: standalone + exclusion group rules combine correctly
11. Expired rule is filtered out
12. Future rule is filtered out
13. Rule with wrong service ID is filtered out
14. Rule with wrong category is filtered out
15. Discount capped at line subtotal (never negative)
16. Priority order is respected (lower priority_level = higher priority)
17. Reasoning object contains all evaluated rules and applied rules

**File: `apps/api/test/pricing.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
1. Create rule with valid input
2. List active rules
3. Compute pricing for order lines
4. Apply to order writes audit trail

IMPORTANT: The PricingEngine class should be a plain class (not @Injectable) or an @Injectable with no DB dependencies — it's pure logic. The PricingService wraps it with DB access.

Follow existing patterns from the codebase. Use `@checc/shared` for types.
The DiscountReasoning type from `@checc/shared/types/pricing.types.ts` should be used for the reasoning JSONB."}

text.text:
All 33 tests pass. Here is a summary of what was created:

## Files Created

**Entities:**
- `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/pricing-rule.entity.ts` -- `@Entity('pricing_rules')` with all specified fields (id, name, description, ruleType, priorityLevel, value as numeric(10,4), minQuantity, applicableServiceIds as uuid array, applicableCategories as varchar array, exclusionGroup, validFrom, validUntil, isActive, createdBy FK to users, createdAt)
- `apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/discount-audit.entity.ts` -- `@Entity('discount_audit_trail')`, immutable (no update methods), with JSONB reasoning column typed as `DiscountReasoning`

**Core Logic:**
- `apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/pricing-engine.ts` -- Pure class (no `@Injectable`, no DB dependencies) implementing the full 9-step algorithm: filter, sort, group by exclusion_group, pick max discount per group with tie-breaking, apply standalone rules, compute discounts by rule type (percentage_off, fixed_off, fixed_price, buy_x_get_y), cap at line subtotal, build reasoning trace
- `apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/pricing.service.ts` -- `@Injectable` service wrapping PricingEngine with DB access: createRule, updateRule, deleteRule (soft-delete), listRules (paginated), computeForOrder (preview), applyToOrder (persist + audit trail), getAuditTrail

**API Layer:**
- `apps/api/src/api/controllers/pricing.controller.ts` -- Six endpoints: POST/PUT/DELETE/GET for rules (Admin only), POST /pricing/compute (any authenticated user), GET /pricing/audit/:orderId (Admin/Staff)
- `apps/api/src/api/modules/pricing.module.ts` -- Wires TypeOrmModule.forFeature with all 5 entities, controller, and service

**Tests:**
- `apps/api/test/pricing-engine.spec.ts` -- 22 tests covering all 17 specified scenarios plus extras
- `apps/api/test/pricing.service.spec.ts` -- 11 tests covering CRUD, compute, apply-to-order with audit trail, and error cases

**Modified:**
- `apps/api/src/app.module.ts` -- Added `PricingModule` import

1	import { z } from 'zod';
2	import { PricingRuleType } from '../types/pricing.types';
3	
4	export const createPricingRuleSchema = z.object({
5	  name: z.string().min(1).max(200),
6	  description: z.string().max(1000).optional(),
7	  ruleType: z.nativeEnum(PricingRuleType),
8	  priorityLevel: z.number().int().min(0),
9	  value: z.number().positive('Value must be positive'),
10	  minQuantity: z.number().int().min(1).optional().default(1),
11	  applicableServiceIds: z.array(z.string().uuid()).optional(),
12	  applicableCategories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
13	  exclusionGroup: z.string().max(100).optional(),
14	  validFrom: z.string().datetime(),
15	  validUntil: z.string().datetime(),
16	}).refine(
17	  (data) => new Date(data.validUntil) > new Date(data.validFrom),
18	  { message: 'validUntil must be after validFrom', path: ['validUntil'] },
19	);
20	
21	export const updatePricingRuleSchema = createPricingRuleSchema.partial();
22	
23	export type CreatePricingRuleInput = z.infer<typeof createPricingRuleSchema>;
24	export type UpdatePricingRuleInput = z.infer<typeof updatePricingRuleSchema>;

content.arguments:
{"description": "Build payments module", "subagent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "Create Module 4: Payments & Refunds for the CHECC project at E:/Hale/Coding/Eaglepoint/Task-54/repo. Modules 0-3 are complete. DO write actual code files.

First read these files to understand existing patterns:
- apps/api/src/app.module.ts
- apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/order.entity.ts
- apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/enrollment.service.ts (to see how it interacts with orders)
- libs/shared/src/types/payment.types.ts
- libs/shared/src/schemas/payment.schema.ts

Then create:

## 1. Entities
**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/payment.entity.ts`**
@Entity('payments') - id, orderId (FK), paymentMethod (cash|check|manual_card), amount, referenceNumber, status (PENDING|PAID|REFUNDED|CANCELED), recordedBy (FK users), paidAt, createdAt, updatedAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/refund.entity.ts`**
@Entity('refunds') - id, paymentId (FK), amount, reasonCode, reasonDetail, requestedBy (FK users), approvedBy (FK users), approvedAt, createdAt

## 2. Service
**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/payment.service.ts`**
- `recordPayment(input, staffUserId)` - Create payment record, mark as PAID, update order status to PAID, trigger enrollment activation. Staff/Admin only.
- `getPaymentsByOrder(orderId)` - List payments for an order
- `getPaymentById(id)` - Get single payment
- `initiateRefund(input, requestingUserId)` - Refund workflow:
  - Check requesting user's can_approve_refunds flag
  - If TRUE: self-approve, process refund immediately
  - If FALSE: require supervisorUsername + supervisorPassword in the request
  - Validate supervisor has can_approve_refunds=true via bcrypt re-auth (use AuthService.verifyCredentials)
  - Create refund record, update payment status to REFUNDED, update order to REFUNDED
  - Require reason_code (from RefundReasonCode enum)
- `listPayments(page, limit)` - For admin view

Import AuthModule to access AuthService for supervisor credential verification.

## 3. Controller
**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/payment.controller.ts`**
- `POST /payments` - Record payment (Staff/Admin) with ZodValidate(recordPaymentSchema)
- `GET /payments/order/:orderId` - Get payments for order
- `GET /payments/:id` - Get payment by ID
- `POST /payments/refund` - Initiate refund (Staff/Admin) with ZodValidate(refundSchema)
- `GET /payments` - List all payments (Admin)

## 4. Module
**`apps/api/src/api/modules/payment.module.ts`**
Import AuthModule. Export PaymentService.

## 5. Update app.module.ts - add PaymentModule

## 6. Tests
**`apps/api/test/payment.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
1. Record payment: creates payment, updates order to PAID
2. Record payment: rejects if order not PENDING_PAYMENT
3. Record payment: rejects if amount doesn't match order total
4. Refund with supervisor flag (self-approve): processes immediately
5. Refund without supervisor flag: requires supervisor credentials
6. Refund with invalid supervisor credentials: rejected
7. Refund with supervisor lacking can_approve_refunds: rejected
8. Refund requires reason code
9. Get payments by order

Follow existing codebase patterns. Use @checc/shared imports. Use WinstonLogger.", "run_in_background": true}

content.arguments:
{"description": "Build health-check module", "subagent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "Create Module 5: Health-Check Reports for the CHECC project at E:/Hale/Coding/Eaglepoint/Task-54/repo. Modules 0-3 are complete. DO write actual code files.

First read these files to understand existing patterns:
- apps/api/src/app.module.ts
- apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/auth.service.ts (for verifyCredentials method)
- apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/user.entity.ts
- libs/shared/src/types/health-check.types.ts
- libs/shared/src/schemas/health-check.schema.ts

Then create:

## 1. Entities
**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/report-template.entity.ts`**
@Entity('report_templates') - id, name, description, sections (JSONB), isActive, createdAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/health-check.entity.ts`**
@Entity('health_checks') - id, patientId (FK), templateId (FK), orderId (FK nullable), status (DRAFT|AWAITING_REVIEW|SIGNED|AMENDED), currentVersion, complianceBreach (boolean), createdBy (FK), createdAt, updatedAt
Relations: OneToMany to HealthCheckVersionEntity

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/health-check-version.entity.ts`**
@Entity('health_check_versions') - id, healthCheckId (FK), versionNumber, contentSnapshot (JSONB), changeSummary, status, createdBy (FK), createdAt
Relations: OneToMany to ResultItemEntity, UNIQUE(healthCheckId, versionNumber)

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/result-item.entity.ts`**
@Entity('health_check_result_items') - id, versionId (FK), testName, testCode, value, unit, referenceLow, referenceHigh, isAbnormal, flag (H|L|HH|LL|C), priorValue, priorDate, sortOrder

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/report-signature.entity.ts`**
@Entity('report_signatures') - id, healthCheckId (FK), versionNumber, signerId (FK users), signatureHash, signedAt, ipAddress

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/report-pdf.entity.ts`**
@Entity('report_pdfs') - id, healthCheckId (FK), versionNumber, filePath, fileSizeBytes, sha256Checksum, generatedAt

## 2. Services
**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/health-check.service.ts`**
- `create(input, staffUserId)` - Create health check with version 1. Populate result items. Auto-detect abnormal flags by comparing value against referenceLow/referenceHigh. Populate prior values by querying patient's most recent prior report for matching test codes.
- `findById(id)` - Get health check with current version and result items
- `findByPatient(patientId, page, limit)` - Paginated list
- `update(id, input, staffUserId)` - Creates a NEW version (increment version number). Only allowed if current version is not SIGNED. If report was SIGNED, creates new version with status DRAFT, header becomes AMENDED.
- `submitForReview(id)` - Transition version to AWAITING_REVIEW
- `getVersionHistory(healthCheckId)` - All versions with their status
- `getTemplates()` - List active templates

**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/signature.service.ts`**
- `sign(healthCheckId, input: {username, password, versionNumber}, ip)` - E-signature:
  1. Validate signer has role='reviewer' 
  2. Re-authenticate via AuthService.verifyCredentials(username, password)
  3. Check version status is AWAITING_REVIEW
  4. Check within 24-hour SLA: version.createdAt + 24h > now
  5. Compute signatureHash = SHA-512(contentSnapshot JSON + signerId + ISO timestamp)
  6. Insert report_signatures row
  7. Update version status to SIGNED, header status to SIGNED
  8. Trigger PDF generation
  
**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/pdf/pdf-export.service.ts`**
- `generateReport(healthCheckId, versionNumber)` - Use PDFKit to generate PDF:
  - Title, patient info, date, template sections
  - Results table with test name, value, unit, reference range, abnormal flag
  - Signature block showing reviewer name and timestamp
  - Save to local path (process.env.PDF_STORAGE_PATH || 'data/pdfs')
  - Compute SHA-256 checksum, store in report_pdfs table
- `downloadReport(healthCheckId, versionNumber)` - Serve file after checksum validation

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/scheduling/compliance-check.service.ts`**
- Cron job: every hour
- Find health_check_versions where status='AWAITING_REVIEW' AND created_at + 24h < NOW()
- Flag parent health_check with compliance_breach=true
- Log as warning

## 3. Controllers
**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/health-check.controller.ts`**
- `POST /health-checks` - Create (Staff only)
- `GET /health-checks` - List (Patient sees own, Staff/Admin sees all)
- `GET /health-checks/:id` - Get by ID with current version
- `PUT /health-checks/:id` - Update (creates new version) (Staff only)
- `POST /health-checks/:id/submit-review` - Submit for review (Staff)
- `POST /health-checks/:id/sign` - Sign (Reviewer only) with ZodValidate(signHealthCheckSchema)
- `GET /health-checks/:id/versions` - Get version history
- `GET /health-checks/:id/pdf/:versionNumber` - Download PDF

**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/template.controller.ts`**
- `GET /templates` - List active templates

## 4. Module
**`apps/api/src/api/modules/health-check.module.ts`**
Import AuthModule for credential verification. Export HealthCheckService.

## 5. Update app.module.ts - add HealthCheckModule

## 6. Tests
**`apps/api/test/health-check.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
1. Create health check with result items
2. Auto-detect abnormal flags (value below low, above high)
3. Update creates new version (increment version number)
4. Cannot update signed version (creates amended version instead)
5. Submit for review transitions status
6. Populate prior values from patient history

**`apps/api/test/signature.service.spec.ts`**
Test cases:
1. Successful sign: valid reviewer, valid credentials, within SLA
2. Reject: user is not a reviewer
3. Reject: invalid credentials (re-auth fails)
4. Reject: version not in AWAITING_REVIEW
5. Reject: past 24-hour SLA window
6. Reject: version already signed
7. Sign generates SHA-512 hash
8. Sign locks version (subsequent updates create new version)

Follow existing codebase patterns. Use @checc/shared imports. Use WinstonLogger. Use crypto for SHA-512/SHA-256.", "run_in_background": true}

content.arguments:
{"description": "Build notifications+content+risk modules", "subagent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "Create Modules 6 (Notifications), 7 (Content Publishing), and 8 (Risk Control) for the CHECC project at E:/Hale/Coding/Eaglepoint/Task-54/repo. Modules 0-5 are complete with 103 passing tests. DO write actual code files.

First read the app.module.ts to see the current imports:
- apps/api/src/app.module.ts

## MODULE 6: NOTIFICATIONS

### Entities
**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/notification.entity.ts`**
@Entity('notifications') - id, userId (FK), type (varchar), title, body, referenceType, referenceId (uuid nullable), isRead (boolean default false), createdAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/notification-delivery-log.entity.ts`**
@Entity('notification_delivery_log') - id, notificationType, referenceId, userId (FK), deliveredAt

### Service
**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/notification.service.ts`**
- `create(input)` - Create notification, check frequency limit: max 3 reminders per (userId, referenceId) per 24h rolling window via notification_delivery_log. If throttled, log warning and skip.
- `findByUser(userId, page, limit, unreadOnly?)` - Paginated
- `markAsRead(id, userId)` - Mark single notification read (object-level auth)
- `markAllAsRead(userId)` - Mark all for user
- `getUnreadCount(userId)` - Count unread
- `canDeliver(userId, referenceId)` - Check frequency limit (used by scheduler)

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/scheduling/notification-scheduler.service.ts`**
- Cron every 30 minutes
- Check for overdue orders (PENDING_PAYMENT with autoCancelAt approaching)
- Check for compliance breaches (health checks flagged)
- Create notifications respecting frequency limits

### Controller
**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/notification.controller.ts`**
- `GET /notifications` - List for current user with optional unreadOnly param
- `PATCH /notifications/:id/read` - Mark read
- `PATCH /notifications/read-all` - Mark all read
- `GET /notifications/unread-count` - Get count

### Module + wire into app.module.ts

### Tests
**`apps/api/test/notification.service.spec.ts`**
- Create notification
- Frequency limit: 4th reminder blocked within 24h
- Mark as read
- Unread count
- Object-level auth: can only read own notifications

---

## MODULE 7: CONTENT PUBLISHING

### Entities
**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/article.entity.ts`**
@Entity('articles') - id, title, slug (unique), body, contentType (article|gallery|audio|video), status (DRAFT|IN_REVIEW|PUBLISHED|REJECTED|ARCHIVED), authorId (FK), reviewerId (FK nullable), reviewNotes, sensitiveWordHits (JSONB nullable), publishedAt, createdAt, updatedAt. ManyToOne to UserEntity for author.

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/media-asset.entity.ts`**
@Entity('media_assets') - id, articleId (FK), filePath, mediaType, mimeType, fileSizeBytes, altText, sortOrder, createdAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/sensitive-word.entity.ts`**
@Entity('sensitive_words') - id, word (unique), severity (HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW), createdAt

### Service
**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/content.service.ts`**
- `create(input, authorId)` - Create article as DRAFT, auto-generate slug from title. Run sensitive word scan on body.
- `update(id, input, userId)` - Update DRAFT article, re-scan for sensitive words
- `submitForReview(id, userId)` - Author submits → IN_REVIEW
- `review(id, reviewData, adminId)` - Admin approves (PUBLISHED + publishedAt) or rejects (REJECTED + reviewNotes)
- `archive(id)` - Set to ARCHIVED
- `listPublished(page, limit)` - Published articles for patients
- `list(page, limit, status?)` - All articles for admin
- `getBySlug(slug)` - Get by slug

**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/sensitive-word.service.ts`**
- `scan(text)` - Load words from DB, build regex patterns, scan text, return [{word, position, context, severity}]
- Context = 20 chars before and after the match

### Controller
**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/content.controller.ts`**
- `POST /content` - Create (Admin/Staff)
- `PUT /content/:id` - Update (author only, DRAFT status)
- `POST /content/:id/submit-review` - Submit for review
- `POST /content/:id/review` - Review (Admin only) with ZodValidate(reviewArticleSchema)
- `POST /content/:id/archive` - Archive (Admin)
- `GET /content/published` - Published articles (any authenticated)
- `GET /content` - All articles (Admin)
- `GET /content/:slug` - Get by slug

### Module + wire into app.module.ts

### Tests
**`apps/api/test/content.service.spec.ts`**
- Create article with auto-slug
- Submit for review transitions to IN_REVIEW
- Admin approves → PUBLISHED
- Admin rejects → REJECTED with notes
- Archive article
- Only author can update their own draft
- Sensitive word detection returns hits

**`apps/api/test/sensitive-word.service.spec.ts`**
- Scan text with matching word returns hit with position and context
- Scan text with no matches returns empty array
- Multiple hits for multiple words

---

## MODULE 8: RISK CONTROL

### Entities
**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/ip-rule.entity.ts`**
@Entity('ip_rules') - id, ipAddress (varchar 45), cidrMask (int default 32), ruleType (allow|deny), reason, createdBy (FK nullable), expiresAt (nullable), createdAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/risk-event.entity.ts`**
@Entity('risk_events') - id, userId (FK nullable), eventType, severity (low|medium|high|critical), details (JSONB), ipAddress, deviceFingerprint, detectedAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/incident-ticket.entity.ts`**
@Entity('incident_tickets') - id, riskEventId (FK), title, description, status (OPEN|INVESTIGATING|RESOLVED|DISMISSED), assignedTo (FK nullable), hitLogs (JSONB), resolvedAt, resolutionNotes, createdAt, updatedAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/captcha-challenge.entity.ts`**
@Entity('captcha_challenges') - id, challengeText, imageData (bytea), expiresAt, isUsed, createdAt

### Guards
**`apps/api/src/api/guards/rate-limit.guard.ts`**
- Check rate_limit_buckets table: sliding 1-minute windows
- Default: 30 req/min/user
- Support @RateLimit() decorator for per-endpoint override
- Returns 429 if exceeded

**`apps/api/src/api/guards/ip-allow-deny.guard.ts`**
- Query ip_rules table (cache with simple Map + 60s TTL)
- If deny match and no allow override → 403
- Applied globally

### Service
**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/risk.service.ts`**
- `createIpRule(input, userId)` - Create IP allow/deny rule
- `deleteIpRule(id)` - Remove rule
- `listIpRules(page, limit)` - Paginated
- `checkIp(ip)` - Returns { allowed: boolean }
- `listRiskEvents(page, limit)` - Paginated
- `listIncidents(page, limit, status?)` - Paginated
- `updateIncident(id, data)` - Update status, assign, resolve

**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/anomaly-detector.service.ts`**
- `checkPromoAbuse(userId)` - Count promotions used in last hour, threshold: 5
- `checkBulkRegistration(ip)` - Count registrations from IP in last hour, threshold: 3
- `checkRepeatedRefunds(userId)` - Count refunds in last 24h, threshold: 3
- Each creates a risk_event + incident_ticket when threshold exceeded

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/security/captcha.service.ts`**
- `generate()` - Create math challenge (e.g., "12 + 7 = ?"), store answer as text, generate a simple text-based representation (no canvas dependency for simplicity), return id + base64 image, 5-min expiry
- `verify(id, answer)` - Check answer, mark used, return boolean

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/security/encryption.util.ts`**
- `encrypt(plaintext, key)` - AES-256-GCM encryption using Node crypto
- `decrypt(ciphertext, key)` - AES-256-GCM decryption
- Key from process.env.FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY

### Decorators
**`apps/api/src/api/decorators/rate-limit.decorator.ts`**
```typescript
export const RATE_LIMIT_KEY = 'rateLimit';
export const RateLimit = (limit: number, windowSeconds = 60) => SetMetadata(RATE_LIMIT_KEY, { limit, windowSeconds });
```

### Controller
**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/risk.controller.ts`**
- `GET /risk/ip-rules` - List (Admin)
- `POST /risk/ip-rules` - Create (Admin)
- `DELETE /risk/ip-rules/:id` - Delete (Admin)
- `GET /risk/events` - List risk events (Admin)
- `GET /risk/incidents` - List incidents (Admin)
- `PATCH /risk/incidents/:id` - Update incident (Admin)
- `GET /risk/captcha` - Get challenge (Public)
- `POST /risk/captcha/verify` - Verify (Public)

### Module + wire into app.module.ts

### Tests
**`apps/api/test/risk.service.spec.ts`**
- Create IP rule
- Check IP: deny rule blocks
- Check IP: allow rule overrides deny
- List risk events
- Update incident status

**`apps/api/test/anomaly-detector.spec.ts`**
- Detect promo abuse above threshold
- No alert below threshold
- Detect bulk registration
- Detect repeated refunds

**`apps/api/test/encryption.util.spec.ts`**
- Encrypt and decrypt round trip
- Decryption with wrong key fails
- Different plaintexts produce different ciphertexts

**`apps/api/test/captcha.service.spec.ts`**
- Generate produces challenge with id and image
- Verify correct answer returns true
- Verify wrong answer returns false
- Verify expired challenge returns false

---

IMPORTANT: 
- Follow existing patterns from auth.module, enrollment.module, etc.
- Use @checc/shared for all types/schemas/constants
- All new modules must be added to app.module.ts imports
- Use WinstonLogger for logging
- Register rate-limit and IP guards globally in app.module.ts as APP_GUARD providers
- Tests must all pass alongside existing 103 tests"}

- `scan(text)` - Load words from DB, build regex patterns, scan text, return [{word, position, context, severity}]
- Context = 20 chars before and after the match

### Controller
**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/content.controller.ts`**
- `POST /content` - Create (Admin/Staff)
- `PUT /content/:id` - Update (author only, DRAFT status)
- `POST /content/:id/submit-review` - Submit for review
- `POST /content/:id/review` - Review (Admin only) with ZodValidate(reviewArticleSchema)
- `POST /content/:id/archive` - Archive (Admin)
- `GET /content/published` - Published articles (any authenticated)
- `GET /content` - All articles (Admin)
- `GET /content/:slug` - Get by slug

### Module + wire into app.module.ts

### Tests
**`apps/api/test/content.service.spec.ts`**
- Create article with auto-slug
- Submit for review transitions to IN_REVIEW
- Admin approves → PUBLISHED
- Admin rejects → REJECTED with notes
- Archive article
- Only author can update their own draft
- Sensitive word detection returns hits

**`apps/api/test/sensitive-word.service.spec.ts`**
- Scan text with matching word returns hit with position and context
- Scan text with no matches returns empty array
- Multiple hits for multiple words

---

## MODULE 8: RISK CONTROL

### Entities
**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/ip-rule.entity.ts`**
@Entity('ip_rules') - id, ipAddress (varchar 45), cidrMask (int default 32), ruleType (allow|deny), reason, createdBy (FK nullable), expiresAt (nullable), createdAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/risk-event.entity.ts`**
@Entity('risk_events') - id, userId (FK nullable), eventType, severity (low|medium|high|critical), details (JSONB), ipAddress, deviceFingerprint, detectedAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/incident-ticket.entity.ts`**
@Entity('incident_tickets') - id, riskEventId (FK), title, description, status (OPEN|INVESTIGATING|RESOLVED|DISMISSED), assignedTo (FK nullable), hitLogs (JSONB), resolvedAt, resolutionNotes, createdAt, updatedAt

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/persistence/entities/captcha-challenge.entity.ts`**
@Entity('captcha_challenges') - id, challengeText, imageData (bytea), expiresAt, isUsed, createdAt

### Guards
**`apps/api/src/api/guards/rate-limit.guard.ts`**
- Check rate_limit_buckets table: sliding 1-minute windows
- Default: 30 req/min/user
- Support @RateLimit() decorator for per-endpoint override
- Returns 429 if exceeded

**`apps/api/src/api/guards/ip-allow-deny.guard.ts`**
- Query ip_rules table (cache with simple Map + 60s TTL)
- If deny match and no allow override → 403
- Applied globally

### Service
**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/risk.service.ts`**
- `createIpRule(input, userId)` - Create IP allow/deny rule
- `deleteIpRule(id)` - Remove rule
- `listIpRules(page, limit)` - Paginated
- `checkIp(ip)` - Returns { allowed: boolean }
- `listRiskEvents(page, limit)` - Paginated
- `listIncidents(page, limit, status?)` - Paginated
- `updateIncident(id, data)` - Update status, assign, resolve

**`apps/api/src/core/application/use-cases/anomaly-detector.service.ts`**
- `checkPromoAbuse(userId)` - Count promotions used in last hour, threshold: 5
- `checkBulkRegistration(ip)` - Count registrations from IP in last hour, threshold: 3
- `checkRepeatedRefunds(userId)` - Count refunds in last 24h, threshold: 3
- Each creates a risk_event + incident_ticket when threshold exceeded

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/security/captcha.service.ts`**
- `generate()` - Create math challenge (e.g., "12 + 7 = ?"), store answer as text, generate a simple text-based representation (no canvas dependency for simplicity), return id + base64 image, 5-min expiry
- `verify(id, answer)` - Check answer, mark used, return boolean

**`apps/api/src/infrastructure/security/encryption.util.ts`**
- `encrypt(plaintext, key)` - AES-256-GCM encryption using Node crypto
- `decrypt(ciphertext, key)` - AES-256-GCM decryption
- Key from process.env.FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY

### Decorators
**`apps/api/src/api/decorators/rate-limit.decorator.ts`**
```typescript
export const RATE_LIMIT_KEY = 'rateLimit';
export const RateLimit = (limit: number, windowSeconds = 60) => SetMetadata(RATE_LIMIT_KEY, { limit, windowSeconds });
```

### Controller
**`apps/api/src/api/controllers/risk.controller.ts`**
- `GET /risk/ip-rules` - List (Admin)
- `POST /risk/ip-rules` - Create (Admin)
- `DELETE /risk/ip-rules/:id` - Delete (Admin)
- `GET /risk/events` - List risk events (Admin)
- `GET /risk/incidents` - List incidents (Admin)
- `PATCH /risk/incidents/:id` - Update incident (Admin)
- `GET /risk/captcha` - Get challenge (Public)
- `POST /risk/captcha/verify` - Verify (Public)

### Module + wire into app.module.ts

### Tests
**`apps/api/test/risk.service.spec.ts`**
- Create IP rule
- Check IP: deny rule blocks
- Check IP: allow rule overrides deny
- List risk events
- Update incident status

**`apps/api/test/anomaly-detector.spec.ts`**
- Detect promo abuse above threshold
- No alert below threshold
- Detect bulk registration
- Detect repeated refunds

**`apps/api/test/encryption.util.spec.ts`**
- Encrypt and decrypt round trip
- Decryption with wrong key fails
- Different plaintexts produce different ciphertexts

**`apps/api/test/captcha.service.spec.ts`**
- Generate produces challenge with id and image
- Verify correct answer returns true
- Verify wrong answer returns false
- Verify expired challenge returns false

---

IMPORTANT: 
- Follow existing patterns from auth.module, enrollment.module, etc.
- Use @checc/shared for all types/schemas/constants
- All new modules must be added to app.module.ts imports
- Use WinstonLogger for logging
- Register rate-limit and IP guards globally in app.module.ts as APP_GUARD providers
- Tests must all pass alongside existing 103 tests"}

## app.module.ts Updates
- Added `NotificationModule`, `ContentModule`, `RiskModule` to imports
- Added `IpAllowDenyGuard` and `RateLimitGuard` as global APP_GUARD providers (before JwtAuthGuard and RolesGuard)

1	# CHECC - Community Health Enrollment & Clinic Commerce System
2	
3	A 100% offline, locally deployed platform for community health clinic management. Supports patient enrollment, service ordering, clinical health-check reports, cultural content publishing, and risk control.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Docker & Docker Compose
8	- Node.js >= 20 (for local development)
9	
10	## Quick Start (Docker)
11	
12	```bash
13	docker compose up --build
14	```
15	
16	This starts:
17	- **PostgreSQL** on port 5432
18	- **API** (NestJS) on port 3000
19	- **Web** (React) on port 5173
20	
21	Database migrations and seed data run automatically on first start.
22	
23	## Local Development
24	
25	```bash
26	# Install all dependencies
27	npm install
28	
29	# Start PostgreSQL (via Docker)
30	docker compose up postgres -d
31	
32	# Run API in dev mode
33	npm run dev:api
34	
35	# Run Web in dev mode (separate terminal)
36	npm run dev:web
37	```
38	
39	## Testing
40	
41	```bash
42	# Run all tests (canonical entrypoint)
43	./run_tests.sh
44	
45	# Run API tests only
46	npm run test:api
47	
48	# Run Web tests only
49	npm run test:web
50	
51	# Run E2E tests (Playwright)
52	npm run test:e2e
53	```
54	
55	## Architecture
56	
57	```
58	repo/
59	├── apps/
60	│   ├── api/          # NestJS REST API (Hexagonal architecture)
61	│   │   └── src/
62	│   │       ├── api/              # Controllers, DTOs, guards, pipes
63	│   │       ├── core/             # Domain entities, use cases, ports
64	│   │       └── infrastructure/   # TypeORM, persistence, scheduling
65	│   └── web/          # React SPA (Vite + shadcn/ui + Tailwind)
66	│       └── src/
67	│           ├── components/       # UI components (shadcn/ui)
68	│           ├── pages/            # Route pages
69	│           ├── stores/           # Zustand state management
70	│           └── api/              # API client layer
71	├── libs/
72	│   └── shared/       # Shared types, Zod schemas, constants
73	├── docker-compose.yml
74	└── run_tests.sh      # Unified test runner
75	```
76	
77	## Roles
78	
79	| Role | Access |
80	|------|--------|
81	| Patient | Browse content, create enrollments, view reports |
82	| Staff | Record health checks, manage payments, process enrollments |
83	| Reviewer | Clinical sign-off on health-check reports |
84	| Admin | Manage pricing rules, content, users, risk alerts |
85	
86	## API Endpoints
87	
88	Base URL: `http://localhost:3000/api`
89	
90	- `GET /health` — Health check
91	- `POST /auth/login` — Authenticate
92	- `POST /auth/register` — Register user
93	- `GET /auth/me` — Current user info
94	- See module-specific documentation in the development plan.
95	
96	## Environment Variables
97	
98	See `.env.example` for all configurable variables.
99	
100	## Seed Users (Development)
101	
102	| Username | Password | Role |
103	|----------|----------|------|
104	| admin | Admin12345678! | Admin |
105	| staff1 | Staff12345678! | Staff |
106	| supervisor | Staff12345678! | Staff (can approve refunds) |
107	| patient1 | Patient12345! | Patient |
108	| reviewer1 | Reviewer12345! | Reviewer |

{"file_path": "E:/Hale/Coding/Eaglepoint/Task-54/repo/README.md", "content": "# CHECC - Community Health Enrollment & Clinic Commerce System

A 100% offline, locally deployed platform for community health clinic management. Supports patient enrollment, service ordering with transparent pricing, clinical health-check reports with e-signature, cultural content publishing, in-app notifications, and comprehensive risk control.

## Prerequisites

- Docker & Docker Compose
- Node.js >= 20 (for local development)

## Quick Start (Docker)

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

This starts:
- **PostgreSQL** on port 5432
- **API** (NestJS) on port 3000
- **Web** (React) on port 5173

Database migrations and seed data run automatically on first start.

## Local Development

```bash
# Install all dependencies
npm install

# Start PostgreSQL (via Docker)
docker compose up postgres -d

# Run API in dev mode
npm run dev:api

# Run Web in dev mode (separate terminal)
npm run dev:web
```

## Testing

```bash
# Run all tests (canonical entrypoint)
./run_tests.sh

# Run API tests only (141 tests across 18 suites)
npm run test:api

# Run Web tests only
npm run test:web

# Run E2E tests (Playwright)
npm run test:e2e
```

## Architecture

**Backend**: Hexagonal/Clean Architecture — domain logic decoupled from framework.
**Frontend**: React SPA with shadcn/ui (Tailwind CSS + Radix UI), Zustand state management.
**Shared**: TypeScript types and Zod schemas shared between frontend and backend.

```
repo/
├── apps/
│   ├── api/                    # NestJS REST API
│   │   └── src/
│   │       ├── api/            # Controllers, guards, pipes, decorators
│   │       ├── core/           # Domain use cases and port interfaces
│   │       └── infrastructure/ # TypeORM, logging, scheduling, security, PDF
│   └── web/                    # React SPA (Vite + shadcn/ui)
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── components/     # shadcn/ui components + domain components
│       │   ├── pages/          # Route pages by module
│       │   ├── stores/         # Zustand stores
│       │   ├── api/            # API client layer
│       │   └── utils/          # Offline storage, fingerprinting
│       └── e2e/                # Playwright E2E tests
├── libs/shared/                # Shared types, Zod schemas, constants
├── docker-compose.yml          # PostgreSQL + API + Web
├── run_tests.sh                # Canonical unified test runner
└── .temp/devplan.md            # Full development plan
```

## Modules

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Auth** | Registration (12+ char passwords), JWT login, account lockout (5 failures/15 min), role-based guards |
| **Enrollment** | Draft→Submitted→Active workflow, service add-ons, seat quotas (60-min reservations) |
| **Orders** | Order creation from enrollment, price snapshots, auto-cancel (30 min, cron every 5 min) |
| **Pricing** | Deterministic best-offer engine, exclusion groups, immutable discount audit trail, line-level reasoning |
| **Payments** | Offline recorded payments (cash/check/card), supervisor-gated refunds with reason codes |
| **Health Checks** | Versioned reports, reference ranges, abnormal flags, reviewer e-signature (re-auth), 24h SLA compliance, PDF export with SHA-256 checksum |
| **Notifications** | Due dates, overdue balances, hold pickups; 3-per-item-per-24h throttle |
| **Content** | Articles/galleries/audio/video, draft→review→publish workflow, regex sensitive-word detection |
| **Risk Control** | IP allow/deny, rate limiting (30 req/min), device fingerprinting, local CAPTCHA, anomaly detection, incident tickets |

## Roles

| Role | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|
| **Patient** | Browse content, create/submit enrollments, view own reports, receive notifications |
| **Staff** | Record health checks, manage payments, process enrollments. `can_approve_refunds` flag for supervisor override |
| **Reviewer** | Clinical sign-off on health-check report versions via e-signature |
| **Admin** | Manage pricing rules, publish/withdraw content, monitor risk alerts, manage IP rules, view incidents |

## API Endpoints

Base URL: `http://localhost:3000/api`

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Description |
|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | Public | Health check |
| `/auth/login` | POST | Public | Login |
| `/auth/register` | POST | Public | Register |
| `/auth/me` | GET | JWT | Current user |
| `/catalog` | GET | JWT | List catalog services |
| `/enrollments` | GET/POST | JWT | List/create enrollments |
| `/enrollments/:id` | GET/PUT | JWT | Get/update enrollment |
| `/enrollments/:id/submit` | POST | JWT | Submit enrollment |
| `/orders` | GET | JWT | List orders |
| `/orders/:id` | GET | JWT | Get order |
| `/pricing/rules` | GET/POST/PUT/DELETE | Admin | Manage pricing rules |
| `/pricing/compute` | POST | JWT | Preview pricing |
| `/pricing/audit/:orderId` | GET | Staff/Admin | Discount audit trail |
| `/payments` | GET/POST | Staff/Admin | List/record payments |
| `/payments/refund` | POST | Staff/Admin | Initiate refund |
| `/health-checks` | GET/POST/PUT | JWT | Health check CRUD |
| `/health-checks/:id/sign` | POST | Reviewer | E-signature |
| `/health-checks/:id/pdf/:v` | GET | JWT | Download PDF |
| `/notifications` | GET | JWT | List notifications |
| `/content` | GET/POST/PUT | JWT | Content CRUD |
| `/content/:id/review` | POST | Admin | Review article |
| `/risk/ip-rules` | GET/POST/DELETE | Admin | IP rules |
| `/risk/events` | GET | Admin | Risk events |
| `/risk/incidents` | GET/PATCH | Admin | Incident tickets |
| `/risk/captcha` | GET/POST | Public | CAPTCHA challenge/verify |

## Security

- Passwords: 12+ characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, special character
- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts → 15-minute lock
- JWT authentication with configurable expiry
- Role-based access control (global guards)
- Object-level authorization (users access only their own data)
- AES-256-GCM field encryption for sensitive data (phone, SSN, medical notes)
- IP allow/deny lists with CIDR support
- Per-action rate limiting (30 req/min default)
- Device fingerprinting (Canvas + AudioContext + hardware)
- Locally generated CAPTCHA challenges
- Anomaly detection: promo abuse, bulk registration, repeated refunds
- PII scrubbed from all logs (Winston structured logging)
- PDFs stored locally with SHA-256 checksum validation

## Environment Variables

See `.env.example` for all configurable variables.

## Seed Data (Development)

| Username | Password | Role | Notes |
|----------|----------|------|-------|
| admin | Admin12345678! | Admin | Full system access |
| staff1 | Staff12345678! | Staff | Standard staff |
| supervisor | Staff12345678! | Staff | Has `can_approve_refunds` flag |
| patient1 | Patient12345! | Patient | Standard patient |
| reviewer1 | Reviewer12345! | Reviewer | Clinical sign-off |

Seed also includes: 8 catalog services, 2 report templates, 3 pricing rules (10% off over $200, BOGO screenings, 50% off second item).
"}

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Auth** | Registration (12+ char passwords), JWT login, account lockout (5 failures/15 min), role-based guards |
| **Enrollment** | Draft→Submitted→Active workflow, service add-ons, seat quotas (60-min reservations) |
| **Orders** | Order creation from enrollment, price snapshots, auto-cancel (30 min, cron every 5 min) |
| **Pricing** | Deterministic best-offer engine, exclusion groups, immutable discount audit trail, line-level reasoning |
| **Payments** | Offline recorded payments (cash/check/card), supervisor-gated refunds with reason codes |
| **Health Checks** | Versioned reports, reference ranges, abnormal flags, reviewer e-signature (re-auth), 24h SLA compliance, PDF export with SHA-256 checksum |
| **Notifications** | Due dates, overdue balances, hold pickups; 3-per-item-per-24h throttle |
| **Content** | Articles/galleries/audio/video, draft→review→publish workflow, regex sensitive-word detection |
| **Risk Control** | IP allow/deny, rate limiting (30 req/min), device fingerprinting, local CAPTCHA, anomaly detection, incident tickets |

## Roles

| Role | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|
| **Patient** | Browse content, create/submit enrollments, view own reports, receive notifications |
| **Staff** | Record health checks, manage payments, process enrollments. `can_approve_refunds` flag for supervisor override |
| **Reviewer** | Clinical sign-off on health-check report versions via e-signature |
| **Admin** | Manage pricing rules, publish/withdraw content, monitor risk alerts, manage IP rules, view incidents |

## API Endpoints

Base URL: `http://localhost:3000/api`

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Description |
|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | Public | Health check |
| `/auth/login` | POST | Public | Login |
| `/auth/register` | POST | Public | Register |
| `/auth/me` | GET | JWT | Current user |
| `/catalog` | GET | JWT | List catalog services |
| `/enrollments` | GET/POST | JWT | List/create enrollments |
| `/enrollments/:id` | GET/PUT | JWT | Get/update enrollment |
| `/enrollments/:id/submit` | POST | JWT | Submit enrollment |
| `/orders` | GET | JWT | List orders |
| `/orders/:id` | GET | JWT | Get order |
| `/pricing/rules` | GET/POST/PUT/DELETE | Admin | Manage pricing rules |
| `/pricing/compute` | POST | JWT | Preview pricing |
| `/pricing/audit/:orderId` | GET | Staff/Admin | Discount audit trail |
| `/payments` | GET/POST | Staff/Admin | List/record payments |
| `/payments/refund` | POST | Staff/Admin | Initiate refund |
| `/health-checks` | GET/POST/PUT | JWT | Health check CRUD |
| `/health-checks/:id/sign` | POST | Reviewer | E-signature |
| `/health-checks/:id/pdf/:v` | GET | JWT | Download PDF |
| `/notifications` | GET | JWT | List notifications |
| `/content` | GET/POST/PUT | JWT | Content CRUD |
| `/content/:id/review` | POST | Admin | Review article |
| `/risk/ip-rules` | GET/POST/DELETE | Admin | IP rules |
| `/risk/events` | GET | Admin | Risk events |
| `/risk/incidents` | GET/PATCH | Admin | Incident tickets |
| `/risk/captcha` | GET/POST | Public | CAPTCHA challenge/verify |

## Security

- Passwords: 12+ characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, special character
- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts → 15-minute lock
- JWT authentication with configurable expiry
- Role-based access control (global guards)
- Object-level authorization (users access only their own data)
- AES-256-GCM field encryption for sensitive data (phone, SSN, medical notes)
- IP allow/deny lists with CIDR support
- Per-action rate limiting (30 req/min default)
- Device fingerprinting (Canvas + AudioContext + hardware)
- Locally generated CAPTCHA challenges
- Anomaly detection: promo abuse, bulk registration, repeated refunds
- PII scrubbed from all logs (Winston structured logging)
- PDFs stored locally with SHA-256 checksum validation

## Environment Variables

See `.env.example` for all configurable variables.

## Seed Data (Development)

| Username | Password | Role | Notes |
|----------|----------|------|-------|
| admin | Admin12345678! | Admin | Full system access |
| staff1 | Staff12345678! | Staff | Standard staff |
| supervisor | Staff12345678! | Staff | Has `can_approve_refunds` flag |
| patient1 | Patient12345! | Patient | Standard patient |
| reviewer1 | Reviewer12345! | Reviewer | Clinical sign-off |

Seed also includes: 8 catalog services, 2 report templates, 3 pricing rules (10% off over $200, BOGO screenings, 50% off second item).
"}

All 10 modules complete. Here's the final summary:

## Implementation Complete

### Codebase Stats
- **180 TypeScript/TSX files** across the monorepo
- **141 unit/integration tests** across 18 test suites, all passing
- **2 Playwright E2E test files** with auth and enrollment flow tests
- **27 TypeORM entities** covering every database table
- **12 controllers**, **13 services**, **8 NestJS modules**

- Clean TypeScript compilation throughout

### Module Coverage vs Original Prompt

| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| Offline enrollment with drafts | Enrollment CRUD + IndexedDB offline storage |
| Add-on services (Lab Panel, Nutrition) | Catalog services + enrollment service lines |
| Transparent "best offer" pricing | Deterministic pricing engine + PriceBreakdown UI |
| Line-level discount reasoning | DiscountReasoning JSONB + DiscountReasonDisplay |
| Mutual exclusion prevention | Exclusion groups with max-discount selection |
| Immutable discount audit trail | discount_audit_trail table (insert-only) |
| Health-check reports with reference ranges | ResultItems with referenceLow/High + abnormal flags |
| Prior result comparison | Prior values populated from patient history |
| Reviewer e-signature (re-auth) | SignatureService with bcrypt re-authentication |
| 24-hour SLA tracking | ComplianceCheckService cron + breach flagging |
| PDF export with checksum | PDFKit + SHA-256 checksum validation |
| Recorded payments (cash/check/card) | PaymentService with method + reference number |
| Auto-cancel after 30 min | OrderTimeoutService cron every 5 minutes |
| Refund with reason + supervisor | Supervisor override with credential re-entry |
| In-app notifications | NotificationService with scheduler |
| 3-reminder-per-item-per-24h throttle | Frequency limiter via delivery log |
| Draft-review-publish content | ContentService state machine |
| Sensitive-word warnings | SensitiveWordService with regex scan + position/context |
| 12+ char passwords + lockout | Zod validation + 5-attempt lockout |
| Device fingerprinting | Canvas + AudioContext + hardware SHA-256 |
| IP allow/deny + rate limiting | Guards with 30 req/min default |
| Local CAPTCHA | Math challenge generation/verification |
| Anomaly detection | Promo abuse, bulk registration, repeated refunds |
| Incident tickets with hit logs | Risk events → incident tickets |
| Field encryption at rest | AES-256-GCM via Node crypto |
| Docker-first | docker-compose.yml with health checks |
| run_tests.sh | Canonical unified test entrypoint |
| shadcn/ui | Full design token system + 8px grid |
